Class UrlHandle

java.lang.Object
uno.anahata.asi.agi.resource.handle.AbstractResourceHandle
uno.anahata.asi.agi.resource.handle.UrlHandle
All Implemented Interfaces:
ResourceHandle, Rebindable
public class UrlHandle extends AbstractResourceHandle
A resource handle that points to a remote URL or a protocol-based entry.

This implementation includes Metadata Caching to prevent 'Connection Storms'. It performs a single efficient check to capture MIME type, existence, and last-modified status in one network round-trip.

Virtual Strategy: This handle represents physical remote content, so it returns false for isVirtual().

Author:
anahata

  • Field Details

    • urlString

      @NonNull private final @NonNull String urlString
      The full URL string for the resource.

      private transient UrlHandle.Metadata cache
      Cached metadata to prevent redundant connections.

  • Constructor Details

    • UrlHandle

      public UrlHandle()

  • Method Details

    • followRedirects

      private HttpURLConnection followRedirects(String targetUrl, String method) throws IOException
      Recursively follows HTTP redirects for the given URL and method.

      This method handles common redirect status codes (301, 302, 303, 307, 308) and updates the target URL from the 'Location' header.

      Parameters:
      targetUrl - The starting URL string.
      method - The HTTP method to use (e.g., HEAD, GET).
      Returns:
      A connected HttpURLConnection pointing to the final destination.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a network error occurs or the redirect limit is reached.

    • refreshMetadata

      private void refreshMetadata()
      Refreshes the metadata cache if it is null by performing a HEAD request. This is a private implementation detail for network efficiency.

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns a user-friendly name for the source.

      Extracts the display name from the URL path or host.

      Returns:
      The source name.

    • getUri

      public URI getUri()
      Gets the unique URI for this resource.

      Returns the URI representation of the URL string.

      Returns:
      The identifier URI.

    • getMimeType

      public String getMimeType()
      Returns the detected MIME type of the resource.

      Triggers a metadata refresh and returns the cached MIME type.

      Returns:
      The MIME type string (e.g., "text/plain", "image/png").

    • getLastModified

      public long getLastModified()
      Returns the last modified timestamp in milliseconds.

      Triggers a metadata refresh and returns the cached modification timestamp.

      Returns:
      The timestamp, or 0 if unknown.

    • exists

      public boolean exists()
      Checks if the resource physically or virtually exists.

      Triggers a metadata refresh and returns true if the remote source is reachable.

      Returns:
      true if the source is available.

    • openStream

      public InputStream openStream() throws IOException
      Opens a fresh input stream to the resource content.

      Opens a fresh input stream to the remote URL.

      Returns:
      A new InputStream instance.
      Throws:
      IOException - if the stream cannot be opened.

    • isVirtual

      public boolean isVirtual()
      Determines if this is a virtual, memory-backed handle.

      High-Fidelity Strategy: Virtual handles (snippets) typically trigger the 'Preview-as-Editor' mode in viewers to ensure constant IDE fidelity without card-swapping.

      Implementation details: returns false as URLs represent persistent remote resources, not memory-backed snippets.

      Returns:
      true if virtual (in-memory content).

    • rebind

      public void rebind()
      Re-initializes the object's state after deserialization.

      Clears the metadata cache to force a fresh HEAD request on next access.

      Specified by:
      rebind in interface Rebindable
      Overrides:
      rebind in class AbstractResourceHandle

    • length

      public long length()
      Returns the total length of the resource content in characters/bytes.

      Returns the cached content length from the remote server.

      Returns:
      The length, or -1 if the length is unknown or streaming.
